key_metrics: Gross margin of 46.9% ($170.49 million gross profit)
Operating income of $37.01 million, up from $1.80 million YoY
Total cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities of $1.53 billion
qoq_changes: Implied revenue increased 2.0% QoQ ($363.15M in Q2 vs implied $356.08M in Q1)
Implied net income increased 24.6% QoQ ($37.05M in Q2 vs implied $29.73M in Q1)
Current debt surged to $631.18M from $101.29M at year-end 2024, reflecting a shift from long-term debt
new_risks: Significant near-term debt maturity, with current debt increasing by $529.89 million since December 31, 2024
Restructuring and asset impairment charges increased to $3.32 million from $1.17 million YoY
management_tone: Bullish, driven by strong top-line growth, massive operating income expansion, and robust liquidity to handle upcoming debt maturities.
analyst_note: Enphase delivered a highly profitable quarter with revenue up 19.7% YoY and net income surging 242%, showcasing excellent operating leverage and cost control. The primary balance sheet development is the reclassification of over $500 million in long-term debt to current liabilities, though their $1.53 billion in cash and marketable securities provides a strong safety net for repayment or refinancing.
